county_metadata.record_losses: Courthouse fire November 23, 1865 caused partial interior destruction. Exterior walls survived and some records were salvaged. Most deed and will records survived, but some court records were lost.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Rowan 1822. Pre-1822 records in Rowan County. Rowan was formed from Anson 1753. For Piedmont Moravian families, also check Forsyth County and Moravian Archives in Winston-Salem.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds and wills — good coverage from 1822. Davidson County Historical Museum in Lexington has local collections. The Genealogical Society of Davidson County publishes research materials. Census records complete from 1830.

source_profiles.source_name: Davidson County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Searchable deed index and images from 1823 to present. Free online access.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Deeds survived the 1865 courthouse fire largely intact.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Deeds and indexes from county formation through 1969.
Digitized microfilm. Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1822 and registers from 1851. Good coverage for entire county history.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Records intact from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Rowan 1836. Pre-1836 records in Rowan County. For families on the Yadkin River boundary, also check Iredell and Forsyth counties.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with the Davie County ROD online portal — free access to deeds from 1837. FamilySearch has deeds, wills, and tax lists. The Davie County Historical and Genealogical Society is active. Census records from 1840.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: free searchable index and images from 1837

source_profiles.source_name: Davie County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Free online access to deed index and images from 1837 to present. One of the better small-county ROD portals.
Digitized microfilm. Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation.
Microfilm. Tax lists useful for tracking property ownership and household composition between census years.
Marriage bonds from 1837 and registers from 1851.

county_metadata.record_losses: Not a burned county, but many court records are missing for unknown reasons. Pre-1784 court minutes are missing or extremely fragmentary. A 1778 legislative act was needed to recover records held by former registrar James Sampson.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Parent of Sampson County (1784). CRITICAL: When Sampson was formed in 1784, it RETAINED the original Duplin County deed books. Copies now available at Duplin ROD and online. Pre-1784 Sampson families must be searched in Duplin records. Duplin fell under Wilmington District Superior Court.
county_metadata.research_tips: The Duplin ROD website is exceptional — nearly all property books from 1749/1750 digitized and downloadable. The searchable Land Grant Map database with USGS overlay is unique and valuable. McGowen's 'Flashes of Duplin's History and Government' is free at ECU digital. Terry Moore's research guide (free PDF at ncgenealogy.org) is essential reading. Lost Souls Genealogy Duplin page has massive volunteer-transcribed records. Over 900 cemeteries documented by NCGenWeb project.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: images and indexes — nearly all property record books from 1749/1750, plus searchable land grant map with USGS overlay

source_profiles.source_name: Duplin County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Nearly all property record books from 1749/1750 digitized and downloadable. Exceptions: books 20, 22, 33, 43, 106, 249, 276, 318 (in-person only). Indexes 1784-present searchable online. Over 2,000 delayed birth certificates searchable. Searchable Land Grant Map database with USGS map overlay.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Original pre-1784 deed books went to Sampson County at formation; copies at Duplin.

source_profiles.source_name: Duplin County Wills and Estate Papers 1663-1978
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Catalog #456898. Microfilm of originals at State Archives.

source_profiles.source_name: Duplin County Baptist Church Records Collection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Concord Baptist (1813-1880), Island Creek Baptist (1802-1984), Kenansville Baptist (1837-1958), Magnolia Baptist (1850-1953), Grove Presbyterian (1848-1890). Chinquapin Baptist (1860-1932) transcribed at duplin.lostsoulsgenealogy.com.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era church records before 1800 do not survive in complete form.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: 1,891 issues on DigitalNC (free). 12,969 scans on OldNews.com (subscription). Best-digitized county newspaper in this region.
Over 900 cemeteries documented with photographs.

source_profiles.repository: Lost Souls Genealogy / NC State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: 1777 Oath of Allegiance viewable online. Extensive roster of Revolutionary soldiers. Militia authorized Sept 9, 1775 under Col. James Kenan.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Records intact from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1881 from Orange AND Wake counties. Pre-1881 records split between Orange County (western portion) and Wake County (eastern portion). Durham-Orange Genealogical Society publishes 'The Trading Path' journal.
county_metadata.research_tips: Pre-1881 research requires checking both Orange and Wake county records depending on location. FamilySearch has land records extending back to 1798 via predecessor counties. Durham County Library North Carolina Collection has local research materials. Duke University libraries also hold significant local history materials.

source_profiles.source_name: Durham County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online index and images from county formation. Searchable database.
Digitized microfilm. Includes some pre-formation records from Orange and Wake counties that cover the Durham area.
Digitized microfilm. Includes some pre-formation wills that were transferred to Durham County.
Records from the Raleigh sub-district covering the Durham area. Labor contracts, marriage registrations, school reports. Critical for African American genealogy.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Exceptional preservation for a colonial-era county.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Bertie 1741. Important colonial county — parent of Halifax (1758), Nash (1777), and indirectly many Piedmont counties. Pre-1741 records in Bertie County. Pre-1759 Edgecombe deeds found in Halifax County deed books 1-6 (Edgecombe Precinct records).
county_metadata.research_tips: Exceptional early records. Deeds from 1732 (as Edgecombe Precinct in Halifax County books). Wills from 1758 across 10+ books. Marriage records from 1760. Court minutes from 1744. Braswell Memorial Library Kornegay Room in Rocky Mount has local history. For pre-1741, check Bertie County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: office info and limited online access

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Includes pre-county-formation deeds from 1732 (Edgecombe Precinct period, originally recorded in Halifax County deed books 1-6). Deeds proper from 1759.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Pre-1759 records in Halifax County deed books.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Will books spanning 10+ volumes. Inventories and accounts included.
Marriage bonds from 1760 and registers from 1851. Colonial-era bonds are significant for eastern NC genealogy.
Court of Pleas and Quarter Sessions minutes from 1744. Among the most complete colonial court records in NC.
Multiple Tarboro newspapers digitized. The Tarboro Southerner (1852+) and earlier papers provide obituaries, legal notices, and community news.
